guide.title: Start your operational model with assets
guide.summary: Assets describe what the organization needs to protect, operate, or recover.
body: List important systems, datasets, services, and business resources as assets.
Later records such as risks and continuity plans can point back to these assets.

concepts.label: Asset
concepts.summary: A business, technical, or information item that the organization needs to manage and protect.
concepts.why_it_exists: Assets give the rest of the compliance model a concrete subject. Risks, controls, and recovery work are easier to understand when they point to the thing that matters.
how_to_use: Create assets for important systems, data sets, services, suppliers, or critical business resources.
Keep ownership, lifecycle state, and descriptive fields current.

relations.summary: Risks can describe threats or weaknesses affecting one asset.
Controls can support how an asset is protected or governed.
Services and plans may depend on the asset.
